item.title: 3 more die with measles-like symptoms
item.description: Three more children have died from measles-like symptoms across Bangladesh in the last 24 hours ending Tuesday morning, according to the Directorate General of Health Services......

item.title: BGB foils 8 more push-in bids amid DG-level talks
item.description: The Border Guard Bangladesh on Tuesday foiled eight more push-in attempts by the Indian Border Security Force across various border points in Kurigram and Jamalpur districts amid ongoing director general level talks between the two forces in New Delhi, India......

item.title: PM urges peace, humanity for safer world
item.description: Prime minister Tarique Rahman has called for upholding the values of peace, humanity and work together to build a better and safer world......

item.title: Tribunal charges lone accused
item.description: The Chattogram Metropolitan Violence against Children Prevention Tribunal on Tuesday framed charges against the lone accused Monir Hossain in the case of raping a four-year-old girl in the Bakalia area of the port city on May 21......

item.title: Jamaat proposes Tk 8.39 lakh crore shadow budget
item.description: Bangladesh Jamaat-e-Islami on Tuesday proposed a Tk 839,505 crore shadow budget, ahead of the national budget for the financial year 2026-27, placing emphasis on public administration, education, technology, and debt servicing......

item.title: BNP, Jamaat face off in JS over Islami Bank
item.description: A legislative face-off erupted in the Jatiya Sangsad on Tuesday as the ruling Bangladesh Nationalist Party and the opposition Jamaat-e-Islami argued over the management, ownership, and political identity of Islami Bank Bangladesh......

item.title: Tigers end 21 years of wait
item.description: Mosaddek Hossain’s all-round heroics on his return and Nahid Rana’s four-for were the highlights as Bangladesh beat Australia by 86 runs by D/L method in their first ODI at the Sher-e-Bangla National Stadium in Mirpur on Tuesday......

item.title: SMEs, creative ventures to get spl focus
item.description: Small and medium entrepreneurs, start-ups, women entrepreneurs, creative economic activities and overseas job seekers are likely to receive special focus in the 2026–27 financial year national budget to be announced tomorrow......

item.title: Myanmar border mines threaten Bangladesh security
item.description: Yet another landmine explosion along Bangladesh-Myanmar border killed a man on Tuesday, starkly exposing Bangladesh’s border risks and a troubling lack of readiness with the authorities to secure the frontier......

item.title: India’s push-in policy, not bilateral, but int’l concern
item.description: DESPITE Bangladesh’s repeated assertion that it is willing to take back any of its citizens found to be residing illegally in India through established legal channels, India’s continued push-in policy has not only fuelled an escalating humanitarian crisis along the border but also placed additional strain on bilateral relations at a time when both countries have significant stakes in......

item.title: State responsiveness vital for public health care
item.description: INTERN doctors’ work abstentions across the country have once again exposed long-standing disputes over allowances and workplace conditions in the health sector. Intern doctors in several districts continued an indefinite work abstention for the second consecutive day on June 8 which affected services in emergency departments, wards and outpatient units. They have......

item.title: Push-ins threaten more than bilateral relations
item.description: EVERY nation has borders. Every state has the right to regulate migration. Every government has a duty to identify undocumented foreign nationals and return them to their countries of origin. These are accepted principles of international relations. Yet how a country performs this task often reveals more about its political priorities than about its immigration policies......

item.title: Justice and public confidence
item.description: OUR news and social media feeds have become harrowing chronicles of vigilantism. Increasingly, the public is taking the law into its own hands, with “mob justice” shifting from an infrequent horror to a recurring spectacle. What was once considered an alarming exception is gradually becoming normalised......

item.title: Budgeting for post-LDC transformation
item.description: BANGLADESH will present its FY2026–27 budget on 11 June amid a challenging macroeconomic environment marked by persistent inflation, constrained domestic revenue mobilisation, rising debt-servicing obligations, weak export growth and pressure on foreign exchange reserves. At the same time, the country is approaching a historic milestone — graduation from......

item.title: Rise of the Global South
item.description: THE humiliating defeat of Israel and the United States in their war on Iran, along with the savagery of the ongoing genocide in Gaza, are ushering in a new world order......

item.title: Bangladesh’s Foreign loans stand at $111b
item.description: Bangladesh’s external debt rose by more than $5 billion year-on-year to $110.93 billion at the end of March 2026, highlighting the government’s growing dependence on foreign borrowing to finance budgetary needs and development projects amid mounting repayment pressures......

item.title: Trump accuses Iran of shooting down US helicopter, vows response
item.description: Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf, Iran’s parliament speaker and its chief negotiator in talks with Washington, meanwhile warned the United States on Tuesday against breaking its ‘commitments’......

item.title: Real Madrid confirm Arbeloa's departure
item.description: Real Madrid confirmed on Tuesday that coach Alvaro Arbeloa was leaving the club, paving the way for the return of Jose Mourinho....

item.title: TIB rejects home minister’s claim of relying solely on media reports
item.description: Transparency International Bangladesh on Tuesday said that it was encouraged by recent developments following its report on the government’s first 100 days, specifically it welcomed the home minister’s comments, discussions in parliament, and the responses from police authorities......

item.title: Bangladesh seeks 1 lakh workers in Russian job market
item.description: Bangladesh and Russia are working to substantially expand manpower cooperation, with Dhaka proposing an increase in the number of Bangladeshi workers in Russia from the current nearly 10,000 to 100,000 within the next year......

item.title: Iran to travel to US day before its first World Cup match
item.description: Iran's football federation announced on Tuesday its national team's schedule for the group stage of the World Cup, a tournament that is beginning under difficult circumstances for the Islamic republic....

item.title: RMG workers block road at Kachukhet
item.description: Workers on Tuesday blocked a road in Kochukhet in Dhaka’s Mirpur area to protest the closure of a garment factory, while layoffs and shutdowns at several readymade garment factories after Eid-ul-Azha sparked demonstrations at multiple locations in and around the capital......

item.title: BADC chair stresses projects’ quality, timely completion
item.description: Bangladesh Agricultural Development Corporation chairman Md Azizul Islam on Tuesday directed project officials to implement development projects within the stipulated timeframe while ensuring the highest quality standards and safeguarding the interests of both the country and the organisation......

item.title: SEU holds workshop on genetic, clinical data management
item.description: The department of pharmacy at Southeast University organised a workshop on ‘Genetic and Clinical Data Management’ under the Higher Education Acceleration and Transformation project to strengthen research capacity in biomedical sciences and improve data management practices recently......

item.title: Prime Bank hosts youth empowerment prog at DIU
item.description: Prime Bank PLC, in collaboration with the Career Development Centre and the DIU Finance Club of Daffodil International University, organised ‘PrimeAcademia Empowering Youth Season 2.0’ on DIU campus in Savar on Tuesday......

item.title: No decision to discontinue honours courses: NU
item.description: The National University said that neither the government nor the university authorities had taken any decision to discontinue any honours, undergraduate, course of the educational institute......

item.title: Somali referee says World Cup 'dream' ruined
item.description: Somali referee Omar Artan said the ‘biggest dream of my life’ had been ripped away after he was denied entry to the United States to officiate at the World Cup....

item.title: Real’s 150 mn euros bid for Alvarez rejected
item.description:
Real Madrid said on Tuesday that Atletico Madrid had rejected the club's offer of 150 million euros ($173 million) for Argentine forward Julian Alvarez.
...

item.title: Activists urge higher cultural budget, integrated initiatives
item.description: Cultural activists in Bangladesh are demanding that the government allocate at least 1 to 2 per cent of the total national budget to the cultural sector, although the sector’s historical allocation usually ranges between only 0.09 to 0.16 per cent......

item.title: Juba Dal leaders among 29 prosecuted
item.description: A case was filed on Tuesday with Ramna police station in connection with Monday’s murder of Bangladesh Jatiyatabadi Swechchhasebak Dal leader Billal Hossain in the capital’s Mouchak area......

item.title: Less than 1pc fishers owns boats, nets, minister tells JS
item.description: Fisheries and livestock minister Amin Ur Rashid on Tuesday told the Jatiya Sangsad that less than one per cent of the country’s fishers owned boats and fishing nets, leaving the vast majority dependent on moneylenders who financed fishing operations......

item.title: Neymar feels like a 'kid' at his last World Cup
item.description: Brazilian star Neymar said he feels like a ‘kid’ ahead of what will be fourth and ‘final’ World Cup, which could yet risk being derailed by a nagging calf injury....

item.title: Madrassah student stabbed to death
item.description: A teenage madrassah student was stabbed to death allegedly over a trivial matter in Aditmari upazila of Lalmonirhat on Monday night......

item.title: Woman, daughter raped in Cox’s Bazar, 6 held
item.description: A 35-year-old woman and her school-going daughter were allegedly gang-raped by a group of robbers while committing robbery at their house in newly formed Matamuhuri upazila of Cox’s Bazar early Tuesday......

item.title: 3rd death anniv of Serajul Alam Khan observed
item.description: The third anniversary of the death of Serajul Alam Khan, one of the key organisers of the country’s War of Independence, was observed on Tuesday......

item.title: Russian strikes kill three in Ukraine
item.description: Russian strikes on Ukraine overnight killed at least three people, including a pregnant woman, Kyiv said Tuesday, hours after president Volodymyr Zelensky held talks with allies in London......

item.title: Edn share in GDP to go up
item.description: The share of education as proportion of the gross domestic product in the forthcoming national budget for 2026–27 financial year is slated to be increased to 2 per cent amid concern over a proper utilisation of the allocation......
